records.primary_purpose: Org-wide GitHub configuration repository for the AFI Protocol organization, holding the org profile/repository map plus a README describing intended reusable GitHub Actions workflows and templates (none of which are actually present in the repo yet).
records.classification_rationale: Contains only README.md, package.json (private, name '.github', description 'Organization-wide GitHub configuration for AFI Protocol'), and profile/README.md (the org repository map). No schemas, contracts, type defs, code, or specs. It is GitHub org tooling/config plus a documentation index, i.e. SUPPORTING infrastructure for the org, not a normative protocol artifact, reference implementation, or research.

records.classification_rationale: This is a static "paper reproducibility bundle" (release tag paper-2025-v2.2) accompanying the AFI paper, not a runtime or a normative spec source. repro/README.md:4-5 "It is a validation pack: schemas, example records, Codex manifest, and a deterministic mock replay. It does not include the AFI runtime, scorers, or DAG engine." The schemas it ships are explicitly described as "snapshots" of what appears in the paper Appendix (repro/README.md:8 "minimal JSON Schemas for snapshots shown in Appendix A"), and the replay tool is a stub (repro/tools/replay/afi-replay.js:2 "Deterministic replay stub (replace with your implementation)"). It supports/documents the protocol (citation, DOI, reproducibility evidence) but is not itself the authoritative schema/spec home (that is afi-config). Not NORMATIVE (snapshot copies, not the canonical schema source), not REFERENCE_IMPL (no working spine — mock_score is a hash, not real scoring), not pure DOCS (it ships runnable schemas/scripts/CI), not STALE (actively the canonical v2.2 release, last commit 2026-01-07).
records.primary_purpose: Versioned, citable (Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.16857347) reproducibility bundle for the "Agentic Financial Intelligence" paper, packaging snapshot JSON Schemas (USS ingestion, vaulted TSSD, universal proposal), an example codex replay manifest, sample vault-ready records, and a deterministic mock-replay smoke test so reviewers can verify schema compliance and replay wiring without the full AFI engine.
protocol_touchpoints: DAG/pipeline (referenced as out-of-scope; pipeline field in schema)
schemas (USS, vaulted TSSD, universal proposal snapshots)
registries/reputation (validators/scorers listed in codex; proposal voting/governance schema)
normative_artifacts: /home/user/AFI-Protocol/afi-artifacts/repro/schemas/universal_signal_ingestion.schema.json (snapshot of USS ingestion, NOT the canonical afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1)
/home/user/AFI-Protocol/afi-artifacts/repro/codex/afi-codex.json (replay manifest: epoch, scorers, validators, seed, deterministic)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: // Canonical validator spec is the strict Zod schema in the paper.

contradictions.evidence: package.json:4 "description": "AFI build artifacts and compiled outputs"; AGENTS.md:3 "It contains repro bundles, compiled binaries, and deterministic build outputs" vs README.md:1 "AFI Artifacts — Paper Reproducibility Bundle"

contradictions.title: Ships its own snapshot copies of USS/vault/CPJ schemas separate from the canonical afi-config source
contradictions.detail: The bundle carries forked snapshot schemas (universal_signal_ingestion, vaulted_tssd, universal_proposal) described as 'snapshots shown in Appendix A'. These can drift from the normative afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1 and vault.schema.json with no linkage/version pin back to the canonical source, risking divergent USS/TSSD definitions across the org.
contradictions.evidence: repro/README.md:8 "schemas/ — minimal JSON Schemas for snapshots shown in Appendix A (A.1/A.2/A.3)"; repro/schemas/universal_signal_schema.mjs:3 "Canonical validator spec is the strict Zod schema in the paper."

contradictions.title: Two divergent codex replay manifests; the example one is unchecksummed and inconsistent with the canonical one
contradictions.detail: repro/codex/afi-codex.json (epoch 2025-08-10, scorers signal-scorer@v0.3.1, validators validator-a/b) diverges from repro/codex/examples/.afi-codex.json (epoch 2025-08-12, scorers SignalScorer/1.0, validator validator:agent_mcp_001). The examples copy is NOT listed in SHA256SUMS, so the pinned-version replay context is ambiguous and partly unverified.
contradictions.evidence: repro/codex/afi-codex.json:2-8 "epoch":"2025-08-10...","scorers":["signal-scorer@v0.3.1"] vs repro/codex/examples/.afi-codex.json:2-4 "epoch":"2025-08-12...","scorers":["SignalScorer/1.0"],"validators":["validator:agent_mcp_001"]

contradictions.title: Reproducibility/integrity guarantee covers only a subset of the bundle; replay logic itself is unverified
contradictions.detail: SHA256SUMS pins only 7 files (3 schemas, 2 examples, codex twins, smoke-test md). The scripts that actually run in CI and produce the replay output — mock_replay.js, canonicalizeSignal.js, the .mjs schemas, sample_tssd.jsonl, and check-repro.sh — are NOT checksummed. A repo that markets byte-for-byte reproducibility leaves its determinism-bearing code outside its own integrity manifest.
contradictions.evidence: repro/SHA256SUMS:1-8 (only codex/, data/examples/{valid-proposal,vault-ready-record}, schemas/{3 json}, tests/replay_smoke_test.md); comm shows tests/mock_replay.js, tools/replay/*.js, schemas/*.mjs, data/sample_tssd.jsonl NOT in SHA256SUMS

contradictions.title: Determinism rule met only by a hash mock, not by pinned-transform scoring
contradictions.detail: REPRODUCIBILITY.md asserts strong determinism (byte-for-byte, seeded), but the only executed 'replay' is SHA256(line+epoch) producing a mock_score; the codex seed=42 is never consumed, and no real pinned scorer/validator versions are exercised. This is honestly disclosed but means the bundle does NOT actually demonstrate the protocol's determinism rule (same inputs+pinned rules => same scored outputs).
contradictions.evidence: repro/tests/mock_replay.js:17-18 "crypto.createHash('sha256').update(line + String(codex.epoch))... score = (parseInt(h.slice(0,8),16) % 10000)/10000"; REPRODUCIBILITY.md:120-123 "Mock Scoring: the mock_score is a deterministic hash, not a real signal score"

contradictions.title: Inconsistent score-scale across example records (0-1 vs 0-100)
contradictions.detail: vault-ready-record.jsonl uses fractional scores (overall 0.78) while sample_tssd.jsonl uses 0-100 (overall 89). The vaulted_tssd schema types score.* only as 'number', so both pass, but the canonical scale is unspecified — an external validator cannot infer the normative score range from these artifacts.
contradictions.evidence: repro/data/examples/vault-ready-record.jsonl:1 "score":{"overall":0.78,...} vs repro/data/sample_tssd.jsonl:1 "score":{"overall":89,"technical":93,...}; repro/schemas/vaulted_tssd.schema.json:124 "overall":{"type":"number"}

upstream: afi-config (canonical schemas/codex/governance: AGENTS.md cites afi-config/codex/governance/droids/AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md; schemas here are snapshots of afi-config USS/vault/CPJ)
AFI paper / SSRN-arXiv (source of the snapshots — schemas labeled 'shown in Appendix A')
downstream: External reviewers / Zenodo citers (the bundle's primary consumers)
AGENTS.md claims consumed by 'CI/CD pipelines, deployment workflows, audit processes'

records.classification_rationale: A standalone Python package (pyproject.toml:6 name="afi-benchkit", entrypoint afi-bench=afi_benchkit.cli:main) for deterministic validator benchmarking. README.md:22-26 explicitly bounds it: "This is a benchmark and verification toolkit only... Does NOT contain DAG/engine/scoring runtime logic... Does NOT contain smart contracts or on-chain code." It defines no protocol schemas, no contracts, no canonical type defs, and is not part of the reference ingest->DAG->vault->mint spine. It produces benchmark metrics (PoI capability, PoInsight usefulness) and a benchmark-local validator "reputation" score that influences selection/allocation, not protocol finality. It is a supporting/verification tool, not NORMATIVE (no spec/schema authority) and not the protocol REFERENCE_IMPL spine.
records.primary_purpose: A standalone, container-packaged Python toolkit that runs deterministic, seeded validator benchmarks (PoI capability and PoInsight usefulness suites) over CSV datasets, emitting hash-stamped canonical artifacts, metrics, calibration/capability plots, and a composite "reputation" score with confidence intervals for reproducible validator verification.
protocol_touchpoints: replay/determinism (hash-locked reproducible benchmark outputs, golden-image verification)
registries/reputation (computes benchmark-derived validator reputation scores influencing selection/allocation)
scoring/metrics (PoI/PoInsight benchmark metrics: IC, hit-rate, Sharpe, coverage, vitality, latency)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: docker run ... ghcr.io/AFI-Protocol/afi-benchkit:stable afi-bench run --suite poinsight ... --seed 1337 ... --emit-scores  (presents a specific org-hosted container image as the canonical run vehicle)
source "$REPO_ROOT/../afi-cli-shared/scripts/lib/afi-shared.sh"  (hard sibling-path dependency on afi-cli-shared org tooling for benchmark generation)
Tier-3 verification - Hash-locked dependencies, bit-for-bit outputs  (a specific determinism/packaging tier is implied as the required verification mode)

contradictions.title: Benchmark 'reputation' score risks being read as protocol-canonical scoring
contradictions.detail: reputation.py computes a composite validator reputation (alpha*PoI + beta*PoInsight) with CIs and an env-tunable composite. The North Star says reputation/registries influence SELECTION & ALLOCATION only and MUST NOT override deterministic scoring or protocol finality. The repo's README does scope it as 'benchmark and verification toolkit only', so this is a presentation/clarity risk rather than a production violation, but the term 'reputation' plus tunable AFI_BENCHKIT_ALPHA/BETA could be mistaken for protocol-level reputation canon.
contradictions.evidence: src/afi_benchkit/reputation.py:65 "reputation_score = alpha * poi_score + beta * poinsight_score"; README.md:112 "Composite Reputation: Weighted combination (alpha=0.3 PoI + beta=0.7 PoInsight)"

contradictions.title: Hardcoded heuristic N=10 and assumed determinism=1.0/uptime=1.0 weaken claimed determinism rigor
contradictions.detail: The reputation bridge fabricates an effective sample size of 10 for PoInsight when canonical CSV is absent, and assumes determinism=1.0 and uptime=1.0 in the PoI raw score (weights are 0 so they do not affect output, but the comment 'Assume deterministic for now' indicates a placeholder). This undercuts the 'deterministic / evidence-weighted' framing for downstream consumers, though it is internal to a benchmark tool, not a protocol scoring path.
contradictions.evidence: src/afi_benchkit/reputation.py:119-120 "Could not determine exact sample size for PoInsight, using estimated N=10"; reputation.py:150 "determinism = 1.0  # Assume deterministic for now"

notable_findings.title: Self-declared boundaries: not protocol runtime, contracts, or DAG
notable_findings.evidence: README.md:22-26 "This is a benchmark and verification toolkit only - Does NOT contain DAG/engine/scoring runtime logic - Does NOT contain smart contracts or on-chain code - Does NOT contain UI components"
notable_findings.recommendation: Accept as SUPPORTING; no protocol-law claims. Keep these boundaries visible in any cross-repo map so it is not mistaken for the scoring DAG or token layer.

notable_findings.title: Determinism is real but local: re-run SHA256 equality + golden-image hashes + fixed timestamp for golden tests
notable_findings.evidence: src/afi_benchkit/tasks/poi01.py:123 "determinism_pass = hash_original == hash_rerun"; src/afi_benchkit/stamp.py:37-38 "if os.environ.get(\"AFI_BENCHKIT_GOLDEN_TEST\") == \"1\": utc_ts = \"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z\""; README.md:13 "Tier-3 verification - Hash-locked dependencies, bit-for-bit outputs"
notable_findings.recommendation: This determinism model (pinned deps via requirements.lock.txt + bit-for-bit golden outputs) is a good candidate pattern to reference in VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC, but it is benchmark-scoped and does NOT implement the protocol's signal replay (no signalId/epoch/vault/TSSD). Tag as partial replay relevance only.

notable_findings.title: Provenance Stamp captures data_hash + git_sha + utc_ts but NOT protocol linkage fields
notable_findings.evidence: src/afi_benchkit/stamp.py:14-19 frozen dataclass Stamp(data_hash, cfg_hash, git_sha, utc_ts); footer format poi01.py:211 "data={short_hash} sha={git_sha} ts={stamp.utc_ts}"
notable_findings.recommendation: Stamp is a benchmark provenance record; it has no signalId/epoch/content-hash anchoring to on-chain commitment. No action; note it is not a replay-critical protocol artifact.

notable_findings.title: No mint/receipt/emissions/vault/TSSD/USS/CPJ touchpoints present
notable_findings.evidence: grep across .py/.md/.toml/.sh/.yml/.json for mongodb|vault|TSSD, usignal|USS|cpj|CPJ, mintForSignal|coordinateMint|beneficiary, AFIMintCoordinator|AFISignalReceipt|EmissionsMinted, kafka|postgresql|timescaledb|influxdb|warehouse -> ZERO hits
notable_findings.recommendation: Confirms this repo touches none of the commitment/evidence/ingest planes; it is orthogonal benchmark tooling. No portable-protocol normative obligations apply.

notable_findings.title: Composite reputation weights are env-tunable (alpha/beta/N0) and recomputed on merge
notable_findings.evidence: src/afi_benchkit/reputation.py:258-263 get_env_overrides() reads AFI_BENCHKIT_ALPHA/BETA/N0; reputation.py:318-324 merge recomputes "reputation_score = alpha * poi_score + beta * poinsight_score"
notable_findings.recommendation: Clarify in README that this 'reputation' is a benchmark-local validator score (selection/allocation input), explicitly NOT protocol reputation canon and NOT authoritative over deterministic scoring or finality, to avoid future conflation with REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION spec.

notable_findings.title: Hardcoded N=10 heuristic and placeholder determinism/uptime in reputation bridge
notable_findings.evidence: src/afi_benchkit/reputation.py:117-120 "For the sample dataset, we know it has 10 rows after cleaning ... using estimated N=10"; reputation.py:150-153 "determinism = 1.0  # Assume deterministic for now ... uptime = 1.0"
notable_findings.recommendation: Fix-in-follow-up: derive N from actual report/CSV in all paths and remove sample-specific magic constant; either implement or remove the unused determinism/uptime terms to avoid misleading evidence-weighting claims.

notable_findings.title: Stale/relative cross-repo source path to afi-cli-shared
notable_findings.evidence: scripts/generate_benchmarks.sh:8 "source \"$REPO_ROOT/../afi-cli-shared/scripts/lib/afi-shared.sh\""; scripts/lock/compile.sh:8 and refresh.sh:8 use "../../afi-cli-shared/..."
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag: benchmark generation/lock scripts assume a sibling-checkout layout of afi-cli-shared; document this hard dependency or vendor the shared lib so the public toolkit is runnable standalone.

notable_findings.title: Reference-suite artifacts mostly untracked; only METADATA.txt committed and references a non-HEAD git SHA
notable_findings.evidence: git ls-files examples/ -> only examples/reference-suite/METADATA.txt; METADATA.txt:3 "Git SHA: 9eb3a14" (HEAD is e3bb1b8); scores.json/plots/metrics are generated, not committed
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag stale: the committed METADATA.txt records a stale generation SHA and the canonical reference-suite outputs it advertises (README.md:51-58) are not in the repo, so the 'canonical reference artifacts' cannot be byte-verified from the checkout; regenerate and either commit or clearly mark as build-time only.

notable_findings.title: Tier-3 strict build enforces hash-locked deps (genuine determinism guarantee)
notable_findings.evidence: Dockerfile:27-28 STRICT=1 -> "pip download --require-hashes -r requirements.lock.txt"; Dockerfile:47-48 "pip install --no-index --find-links=/wheelhouse --require-hashes -r requirements.lock.txt"; Dockerfile:15 PYTHONHASHSEED=0
notable_findings.recommendation: Solid hermetic-build pattern; worth citing as a reference for how reference-impl repos should pin transform/dependency versions for replay, even though this repo is not itself replay-critical to the protocol.

records.primary_purpose: A generic, framework-agnostic Node.js/TypeScript CLI scaffolding library (a Commander.js wrapper providing base CLI app class, hierarchical config, logging, validation, error handling, and an extension/plugin system) intended to be reused by AFI command-line tools.
records.classification_rationale: README.md:3 describes it as "A modular CLI framework for AFI Node.js projects, built on Commander.js" and package.json:3 as "Modular CLI framework for AFI projects using Commander.js". The only dependency is commander (package.json:16). Source (src/) contains CliApp, ConfigManager, Logger, validation helpers, error handling, and an ExtensionManager — pure developer tooling. Targeted searches for every protocol-surface term (signal/USS/CPJ/vault/TSSD/mint/receipt/DAG/replay/determinism/registry/schema/contract/Mongo/etc.) returned ZERO matches across src, README, and config. No normative artifacts, no protocol logic, no chain code. It is shared infrastructure for AFI repos, hence SUPPORTING (not NORMATIVE, REFERENCE_IMPL, or STALE).

notable_findings.title: No AFI protocol surface present anywhere in the repo
notable_findings.evidence: rg over src/, README.md, package.json for mongodb|TSSD|vault|usignal|CPJ|mintForSignal|beneficiary|epoch|replay|determinism|AFIMintCoordinator|pipeline|DAG|orchestrator|warehouse|normative|canonical|postgresql|timescaledb|influxdb -> 'NO MATCHES for protocol patterns'; broader search for signal|schema|mint|emission|registr|reputation|onchain|solidity|web3|ethers|protocol|reactor|gateway -> 'NO MATCHES'.
notable_findings.recommendation: Treat as generic shared tooling. No protocol audit obligations. No spec extraction needed.

notable_findings.title: AFI linkage is branding-only (name, config file, env prefix)
notable_findings.evidence: package.json:2 "name": "@afi/cli-framework"; CliApp.ts:23 new ConfigManager('.afi-cli.json', defaults); ConfigManager.ts:57 if (key.startsWith('AFI_CLI_')). These are the only AFI references; none encode protocol semantics.
notable_findings.recommendation: None. Naming is appropriate for an org-internal utility.

notable_findings.title: Config layering order in README vs implementation is consistent (env overrides file overrides defaults)
notable_findings.evidence: README.md:39-43 lists defaults -> .afi-cli.json -> AFI_CLI_ env; ConfigManager.ts:33-65 applies defaults, then file spread, then env via setNestedValue, matching the documented precedence.
notable_findings.recommendation: None. Behavior matches docs.

notable_findings.title: ConfigManager.save() has unresolved design notes left as inline comments
notable_findings.evidence: ConfigManager.ts:109-119 contains deliberative comments e.g. 'Remove env vars from file config? Or save all?' and 'better to save only non-defaults.' then saves the full merged config including env-injected values.
notable_findings.recommendation: Minor follow-up in the owning repo: saving the env-merged config can persist env-only values into .afi-cli.json. Not protocol-relevant; cosmetic/dev-quality only.

notable_findings.title: Repo is a single 'Initial commit' checkout, no tags/branches
notable_findings.evidence: git log --oneline: single commit '5b46ed4 Initial commit'; remote git@github.com:AFI-Protocol/afi-cli-framework.git.
notable_findings.recommendation: None. Early-stage utility; nothing to tag stale or extract.

records.classification_rationale: This repo is the canonical JSON Schema + spec library that defines the AFI interoperability surface. README.md:3-5 declares it the "single source of truth for all AFI Protocol configuration schemas". It physically holds named normative artifacts: USS v1/v1.1 (schemas/usignal/), CPJ v0.1 (schemas/cpj/v0_1/), vault.schema.json, pipeline.schema.json, blueprint.schema.json, plugin-manifest.schema.json, and the REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md invariants spec. AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:15 correctly self-identifies these as "normative (protocol law)" vs reference implementations. These are the schemas an external validator must conform to, so the repo is NORMATIVE rather than a reference impl.
records.primary_purpose: afi-config is the canonical configuration and JSON Schema library for AFI Protocol: it provides the normative ingest dialects (USS v1.1, CPJ v0.1), vault/pipeline/plugin/validator/blueprint schemas, agent-registry + reputation invariants, and AJV-based validation utilities consumed by every other AFI module.

reference_only_assumptions.quote: **`afi-reactor`** is the orchestrator; do not reintroduce or reference `afi-engine`.
Schema for Time-Series Signal Data Vault configurations in afi-infra
Schema for signal processing pipeline configurations in afi-reactor
Schema for plugin manifests in afi-plugins registry
Schema for analyst configurations in the AFI-Reactor pipeline / DAG integration.
Enrichment nodes are processing units that transform, analyze, or augment signals within the AFI-Reactor pipeline / DAG.
### afi-reactor (DAG Orchestrator)

contradictions.title: Vault schema lacks canonical lifecycle stage enum despite stages being a normative protocol concept
contradictions.detail: The portable-protocol north star defines a per-signal RAW->ENRICHED->ANALYZED->SCORED->MINTED->REPLAYED lifecycle that an external stack must reproduce. vault.schema.json only mentions stages as a free-text index hint and defines no enumerated `stage` field or stage transition rules anywhere in schemas/. The lifecycle is therefore not machine-enforceable from the canonical schema library, weakening interoperability/replay guarantees.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/vault.schema.json:95-98 "stageIndex": {..."description": "Create index on signal stage (RAW, ENRICHED, etc.)"} — no enum/stage field defined elsewhere

contradictions.title: No on-chain commitment schema in the canonical library
contradictions.detail: Vault enumerates Mongo/Postgres/Timescale/Influx (evidence plane, with MongoDB TSSD as the implemented reference store) but the library has zero schemas for the Commitment plane (mint receipts/emissions caps/epoch linkage/content hashes). grep for mintForSignal|EmissionsMinted|AFISignalReceipt returns no schema hits. The commitment plane is not represented in the source of truth, risking collapse of the layer model.
contradictions.evidence: grep kafka|warehouse|snowflake across repo: 0 hits; grep mintForSignal|coordinateMint|EmissionsMinted: only validatorConfig coordinatorAddress + droid-index doc mentions, no schema

contradictions.title: Charter hard-pins afi-reactor as THE orchestrator (reference-impl-as-law)
contradictions.detail: AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md is declared globally binding (AGENTS.md:5 'If this AGENTS.md conflicts with the Charter, the Charter wins') and states afi-reactor IS the orchestrator and forbids referencing alternatives. This collides with AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:15 which calls afi-reactor a 'reference implementation, not mandatory stack'. Within one repo the reference spine is simultaneously declared optional (overview) and mandated identity (charter).
contradictions.evidence: codex/governance/droids/AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md:117 "**`afi-reactor`** is the orchestrator" vs docs/AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:15 "afi-reactor ... are reference implementations—demonstration spines, not mandatory stacks"

contradictions.title: Schema descriptions hard-bind generic schemas to specific org repos
contradictions.detail: Generic, portable schemas (vault, pipeline, plugin-manifest, analyst-config, enrichment-node) describe themselves as belonging to afi-infra / afi-reactor / afi-plugins. An external validator reading the canonical schema cannot tell the rule from the reference deployment, blurring the normative/reference line the protocol depends on.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/pipeline.schema.json:5 "...pipeline configurations in afi-reactor"; schemas/vault.schema.json:5 "...Vault configurations in afi-infra"

contradictions.title: Orphaned .afi-codex.schema.json contradicts the actual codex metadata schema
contradictions.detail: README.md:29 and AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:153 advertise `.afi-codex.schema.json` as the 'Codex metadata schema (all repos)'. But that file is a persona/abilities schema requiring [persona, abilities]; the real .afi-codex.json (codexVersion 2.0.0, module/entrypoints/provides) is validated against schemas/repo-metadata.schema.json instead (test line 141). The advertised codex schema is stale and would reject every real .afi-codex.json.
contradictions.evidence: .afi-codex.schema.json:31-34 "required": ["persona","abilities"] vs tests/schema-validation.test.ts:141 loadJSON('schemas/repo-metadata.schema.json') used to validate .afi-codex.json

contradictions.title: CI never runs schema validation (script name mismatch)
contradictions.detail: The CI gate calls `npm run validate:config --if-present` and `npm run build --if-present`, but package.json defines `validate` and `build`, not `validate:config`. The validate step silently falls through to 'No validation script', so PRs to the single-source-of-truth schema repo merge without the AJV compile/example tests running. Normative schema changes are effectively ungated.
contradictions.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml:21-22 "run: npm run validate:config --if-present || echo \"No validation script\"" vs package.json:13 "validate": "vitest run tests/schema-validation.test.ts"

contradictions.title: Dual USS canon (v1 vs v1.1) without a normative supersession statement
contradictions.detail: USS v1 (provenance requires only timestamp) and USS v1.1 (provenance requires source+providerId+signalId, the audit/reward keys) coexist. vault.schema.json and pipeline.schema.json still default signalSchema to 'afi.usignal.v1', while v1.1 is labeled 'living canon for runtime ingestion'. No doc states which is binding for replay/attribution, so an external implementer could validate against the weaker v1 and omit signalId/providerId.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/usignal/v1/index.schema.json:207-209 provenance required:[timestamp] vs schemas/usignal/v1_1/index.schema.json:235-239 required:[source,providerId,signalId]; vault.schema.json:43 default "afi.usignal.v1"

contradictions.title: Duplicated, divergent USS schemas in afi-uss-pr-v2p1/ staging directory
contradictions.detail: afi-uss-pr-v2p1/ ships a parallel copy of USS v1 schemas + TS types under schema/usignal/v1/ that diverges from the canonical schemas/usignal/v1/ (different $schema draft: 2020-12 vs draft-07, no $id). A second physical 'source of truth' for the same dialect inside the canonical repo invites drift and confuses consumers about which path is authoritative.
contradictions.evidence: afi-uss-pr-v2p1/schema/usignal/v1/core.schema.json:2 "$schema":"https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema" (no $id) vs schemas/usignal/v1/core.schema.json:2-3 draft-07 + $id; diff = DIFFERENT

notable_findings.title: USS v1.1 provenance carries the replay/attribution determinism keys
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/usignal/v1_1/index.schema.json:235-261 provenance required [source,providerId,signalId]; properties ingestHash "SHA256 hash of raw payload ... for integrity verification", facts block (line 33) "replay-canonical market/strategy metadata ... persisted in TSSD vault for deterministic replay"
notable_findings.recommendation: Treat v1.1 index+core as the binding ingest dialect for replay; tests already enforce providerId/signalId rejection (test lines 461-505). Add a doc note marking v1.1 as the canon for replay/attribution and v1 as legacy.

notable_findings.title: Vault schema correctly supports multi-engine (refutes Mongo-only tunnel vision)
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/vault.schema.json:14-23 engine enum ["mongodb","postgresql","timescaledb","influxdb"]
notable_findings.recommendation: Good alignment with portable protocol. Strengthen further by adding an enumerated signal lifecycle `stage` field so the RAW->REPLAYED canon is engine-agnostic and machine-checkable.

notable_findings.title: REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1 codifies the determinism guardrail exactly as the north star requires
notable_findings.evidence: docs/REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md:104-118 "Reputation cannot alter the Unified Weighting Rule (UWR)...", "Vault finality (T.S.S.D. Vault immutability) is absolute; reputation cannot rewrite history"
notable_findings.recommendation: No change; this is the canonical statement of the reputation-MUST-NOT-override-determinism rule. Cross-link it from the portable-protocol surface spec.

notable_findings.title: CPJ v0.1 is a true normative ingest dialect with parser provenance + confidence
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/cpj/v0_1/core.schema.json:5 "CPJ is source-faithful parsed object + provenance + parse confidence"; index.schema.json:5 "first normalization stage before USS v1.1 mapping"
notable_findings.recommendation: Keep. CPJ->USS v1.1 mapping is replay-relevant; ensure the mapping itself (CPJ to USS) is pinned/versioned somewhere normative.

notable_findings.title: CI validation gate is a no-op due to script name mismatch
notable_findings.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml:21-22 run: npm run validate:config --if-present (package.json has no validate:config; defines validate at line 13)
notable_findings.recommendation: Fix CI to call `npm run validate` (and `npm test`) so the AJV schema-compile + example-validation suite gates every PR to the source-of-truth repo. Until fixed, malformed normative schemas can merge to main.

notable_findings.title: Advertised .afi-codex.schema.json is orphaned/stale vs repo-metadata.schema.json
notable_findings.evidence: README.md:29 lists ".afi-codex.schema.json - Codex metadata schema (all repos)"; file requires [persona,abilities] (.afi-codex.schema.json:31-34) but .afi-codex.json is validated against schemas/repo-metadata.schema.json (test:141)
notable_findings.recommendation: Either delete/repurpose .afi-codex.schema.json or repoint README/overview to repo-metadata.schema.json as the actual codex metadata schema. Add a test that validates .afi-codex.json against whatever is advertised.

notable_findings.title: Generic schemas hard-label themselves to specific org repos
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/pipeline.schema.json:5 "...in afi-reactor"; schemas/vault.schema.json:5 "...in afi-infra"; schemas/plugin-manifest.schema.json:5 "...in afi-plugins registry"; schemas/definitions/enrichment-node.schema.json:5 "...within the AFI-Reactor pipeline / DAG"
notable_findings.recommendation: Rephrase descriptions to be stack-neutral (e.g., 'pipeline/DAG configuration for any conforming orchestrator; afi-reactor is the reference implementation'). This is the cheapest, highest-leverage normative/reference clarification.

notable_findings.title: Charter (globally binding) declares afi-reactor THE orchestrator, conflicting with overview
notable_findings.evidence: AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md:117 "afi-reactor is the orchestrator"; AGENTS.md:5 "the Charter wins"; AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:15 afi-reactor is "reference implementation ... not mandatory"
notable_findings.recommendation: Reconcile: scope the Charter's 'reactor is the orchestrator' to the reference deployment/org-internal work, and explicitly state external validators may run any conforming orchestrator that emits USS/TSSD-compliant outputs.

notable_findings.title: No Commitment-plane schema (mint receipt/emissions/epoch/content-hash anchors)
notable_findings.evidence: Only validatorConfig.schema.json:79-103 references mint (coordinatorAddress regex, chainId, gas) and droid docs name AFIMintCoordinator/AFISignalReceipt/EmissionsMinted; no schema defines receipt metadata (signalId/epoch/amounts/beneficiary/contentHash)
notable_findings.recommendation: Add a normative receipt/anchor schema to this library so the on-chain<->off-chain linkage (signalId/epoch/content hash) is validatable by external parties, per decentralization rule 4. Even a breadcrumb-receipt schema would close the gap.

notable_findings.title: Duplicate divergent USS copy under afi-uss-pr-v2p1/
notable_findings.evidence: afi-uss-pr-v2p1/schema/usignal/v1/core.schema.json uses draft 2020-12 and omits $id; canonical schemas/usignal/v1/core.schema.json uses draft-07 with $id; diff returns DIFFERENT
notable_findings.recommendation: Remove the afi-uss-pr-v2p1/ staging directory (or fold useful greeks updates into canonical schemas) so there is one authoritative USS path. It is unreferenced by tests and not under schemas/.

notable_findings.title: validatorConfig encodes mint/governance/challenge-window determinism inputs
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/validatorConfig.schema.json:7-46 required [decayCheckIntervalMs, challengeWindowDurationHours, mintApprovalThreshold, snapshotSpaceId]; mint.chainId comment "8453 for Base"; coordinatorAddress regex ^0x[a-fA-F0-9]{40}$
notable_findings.recommendation: These thresholds are replay/finality-relevant. Ensure validator replay spec pins these values per-epoch so a different stack reproduces the same mint/no_mint decision.

records.primary_purpose: afi-core is a pure-ESM TypeScript runtime library providing the deterministic scoring/validation primitives of AFI Protocol: the canonical UWR (Universal Weighting Rule) scorer, time/Greeks decay integration over afi-math, deterministic novelty scoring, the per-signal AnalystScoreTemplate, the ValidatorDecision/Outcome envelopes, a SignalEnvelope DAG record type, local v0.1 Zod schemas, and stub runtime/ElizaOS adapter types (package.json:3 "Core runtime for agent signal validation in AFI Protocol").
records.classification_rationale: It implements concrete, working scoring/validation logic for the reference spine (UWR aggregator, decay, novelty, Froggy analyst) rather than defining cross-stack interoperability rules, yet it also holds some genuinely normative-adjacent type contracts. It self-describes as "our runtime library, not our boss" and "NOT an orchestrator" (.factory/droids/schema-validator-droid.md:91-92), and explicitly defers canonical schemas to afi-config, contracts to afi-token, orchestration to afi-reactor, and TSSD to afi-infra (AGENTS.md:79-84; schemas/README.md; schemas/universal_signal_schema.backup.ts:13-16). The local SignalSchema is a thin convenience shape, not the normative USS v1.1. Net: a reference implementation of the deterministic scoring layer with embedded canonical TS type definitions.
protocol_touchpoints: schemas (local v0.1 Zod: SignalSchema, PipelineConfig, ValidatorMetadata, ValidatorGovernance, SignalFinalizationRequest, AnalystScoreTemplate, GreeksDecayTemplate)
replay/determinism (UWR + novelty deterministic; ValidatorDecisionBase.createdAt for audit/replay; replaySessionId)
registries/reputation (ValidatorMetadata poiScore/stakeWeight/reputationScore; ValidatorGovernance; explicit rule reputation MUST NOT override UWR/vault finality)
mint/receipt (ValidatorOutcome.mintEligible; emitMintInstruction stub; docs reference AFIMintCoordinator/AFISignalReceipt as out-of-repo)
TSSD/vault (referenced only as out-of-repo finality target; afiRuntimeAdapter telemetry-to-vault TODOs)
USS/CPJ ingest (regimeTag aligns to macro.regimeTag in uSignal schema owned by afi-config; no CPJ)
SDK/API (package exports of validators/schemas/analyst/decay/runtime for downstream consumers)
emissions (referenced only; off-chain emissions docs note splits/Epoch Pulse unimplemented)
normative_artifacts: /home/user/AFI-Protocol/afi-core/validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts (canonical UWR v0.1 implementation + UwrAxesInput/Config type contract)
/home/user/AFI-Protocol/afi-core/src/analyst/AnalystScoreTemplate.ts (canonical per-signal analyst scoring template + Zod schema)
/home/user/AFI-Protocol/afi-core/docs/VALIDATOR_DECISION_SPEC.v0.1.md (implementation-adjacent stub; defers canonical spec to afi-docs)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: AFI-Reactor (already hardened) is the **canonical orchestrator** for AFI Protocol.
AFI-Core is the **runtime layer powered by ElizaOS** (or a future soft fork thereof).
DAG Topology / Policy - That's afi-reactor (15-node DAG, pipeline definitions, orchestration rules)
afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI — afi-core is NOT an orchestrator.
Load ElizaOS runtime ... Connect to T.S.S.D. Vault for telemetry
ElizaOS-based runtime layer for AFI, bridging AFI-Reactor DAG plans to agents and mint instructions

contradictions.title: Reactor presented as THE canonical orchestrator (reference spine asserted as protocol law)
contradictions.detail: Per the portable-protocol north star, afi-reactor is the reference DAG implementation, with any conforming orchestrator (if outputs conform) as a pluggable alternative. afi-core docs and droid doctrine elevate afi-reactor to 'the canonical orchestrator' with a fixed '15-node DAG' as if it were normative, which can drive wrong architectural decisions about whether the DAG framework is swappable.
contradictions.evidence: docs/AFI_CORE_RUNTIME_OVERVIEW.md:39 'AFI-Reactor (already hardened) is the **canonical orchestrator** for AFI Protocol'; :29 'DAG Topology / Policy - That's afi-reactor (15-node DAG ...)'; .factory/droids/schema-validator-droid.md:91 'afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI'

contradictions.title: ElizaOS framed as the runtime/soft-fork target (specific stack implied mandatory)
contradictions.detail: afi-core is framed as 'the runtime layer powered by ElizaOS' destined to become an ElizaOS soft fork, and .afi-codex describes it as 'ElizaOS-based runtime layer'. Portable protocol treats agent runtime as pluggable; baking ElizaOS into core identity risks presenting one runtime stack as protocol-mandatory. (Mitigated: AGENTS.md:89 forbids importing ElizaOS into core, and no ElizaOS dep exists yet.)
contradictions.evidence: docs/AFI_CORE_RUNTIME_OVERVIEW.md:10 'runtime layer powered by ElizaOS (or a future soft fork thereof)'; :163 'AFI-Core will evolve into a **soft fork of ElizaOS**'; .afi-codex:3

contradictions.title: Local SignalSchema diverges from normative USS v1.1 surface
contradictions.detail: schemas/universal_signal_schema.ts is a flat trading-signal shape (symbol/action/content<=280 chars/indicators) with no macro lens, no provenance/CPJ, no signalId/epoch/content-hash linkage required by the USS v1.1 normative surface. Code/docs reference macro.regimeTag and the canonical uSignal schema as living in afi-config, so this local schema is a partial convenience shape that should not be mistaken for canonical USS.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/universal_signal_schema.ts:3-39 (no macro/provenance/epoch fields; content z.string().min(1).max(280)); validators/ValidatorDecision.ts:104 'aligned with macro.regimeTag in usignal schema'; docs/VALIDATOR_DECISION_SPEC.v0.1.md:12 'macro.regimeTag from the uSignal schema (afi-config)'

contradictions.title: AGENTS.md documents many npm scripts that do not exist (replay/determinism + validation commands absent)
contradictions.detail: AGENTS.md instructs running npm run validate-all, simulate-signal, simulate-from-vault, replay-vault, codex-lint, mentor-eval. None of these exist in package.json (only build, typecheck, test, esm:check). The 'replay-vault for determinism testing' command in particular is advertised but unimplemented, weakening the repo's stated replay/determinism posture.
contradictions.evidence: AGENTS.md:36-63 'npm run validate-all ... npm run simulate-signal ... npm run replay-vault # Replay vault for determinism testing'; package.json:59-67 scripts only contain build/build:clean/prepare/typecheck/test/test:run/esm:check

contradictions.title: Conflicting dependency declarations across the two codex manifests
contradictions.detail: .afi-codex.json lists dependsOn [afi-config, afi-math] (correct: real dep is @afi-protocol/afi-math, schemas defer to afi-config), while .afi-codex lists dependsOn [afi-reactor, afi-token]. afi-core depending on afi-reactor/afi-token would invert the documented dependency direction (consumers are afi-reactor/afi-plugins). The .afi-codex declaration contradicts AGENTS.md and the actual package.json deps.

records.classification_rationale: This is the AFI documentation hub. README.md:6 states it is "the official documentation hub for every public facet of AFI: protocol specs, developer guides, governance blueprints, and the illustrative Signal-Lore series." AGENTS.md:13-16 explicitly forbids code ("What this repo is NOT for: Code implementation (use afi-core, afi-reactor, etc.)"). The tree contains only Markdown/JSON config (no .ts/.sol sources). Notably it is ALSO the canonical home of the portable-protocol north star spec itself (specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md) and the investigation charter (specs/AFI_PROTOCOL_INVESTIGATION_PROMPT.md), but those are documents, not enforced artifacts; the repo as a whole is DOCS. It contains a mix of current direction-setting docs and several stale/contradictory legacy docs flagged by the spec.
records.primary_purpose: Documentation hub for AFI Protocol: hosts the portable-protocol surface spec and investigation charter, plus developer guides, architecture/status docs, branch/audit reports, droid instructions, and the Signal-Lore narrative series; contains no protocol code.

reference_only_assumptions.quote:
### 1. afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI
All canonical pipelines, DAGs, and routing logic live here—not in afi-core, not in random helpers.
Repository: afi-reactor  Purpose: Canonical orchestration principles for AFI Protocol  Status: Authoritative

### 3. The DAG is law
Every signal path (ingest → enrich → score → mint/review) must be expressible as a Reactor DAG; ad-hoc flows are anti-patterns.
- ✅ MongoDB TSSD vault persistence (Phase 1 complete)
`afi-infra` — TSSD vault (MongoDB), types, schemas
afi-reactor: TSSD vault persistence via src/services/tssdVaultService.ts; writes canonical USS + pipeline outputs when AFI_MONGO_URI configured
Purpose: Single source of truth for how AFI orchestration is implemented today.

contradictions.title: Orchestrator Doctrine declares reactor THE protocol orchestrator and is labeled Authoritative, contradicting reference-only framing
contradictions.detail: A full copy of AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md lives in afi-docs root, headed 'Status: Authoritative' and 'This doctrine is authoritative.' It states afi-reactor 'is the orchestrator of AFI' and 'The DAG is law' (every signal path MUST be a Reactor DAG). The portable-protocol spec explicitly lists this doctrine as a tension to reframe: reactor should be the REFERENCE orchestrator, with protocol law being conforming DAG outputs + pinned versions. Having an 'Authoritative' reactor-only doctrine in the docs hub directly works against the portable direction.
contradictions.evidence: AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:5,11,38-40,290 'Status: Authoritative'; '#1 afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI'; '#3 The DAG is law ... must be expressible as a Reactor DAG'; 'This doctrine is authoritative. All afi-reactor code must comply.' vs specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:133 'Reframe: reactor is reference orchestrator'

contradictions.title: Pipeline audit correctly documents MongoDB TSSD vault as the canonical/working evidence store
contradictions.detail: AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md consistently identifies the TSSD vault with MongoDB ('MongoDB TSSD vault persistence (Phase 1 complete)', 'afi-infra — TSSD vault (MongoDB)', 'Storage: MongoDB time-series collection', AFI_MONGO_URI). This matches the implemented reference store: MongoDB TSSD is the canonical evidence store for scored signals. The portable spec's vault.schema.json additionally allows PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB/InfluxDB as engine substitutes that conform to the same schema; the doc could note Mongo is the default reference engine rather than a hard requirement.

contradictions.title: Repo models multi-role econ splits (gauge) while reference mint path is single-beneficiary
contradictions.detail: This research kit models allocation across producers/enrichment/validators/public_goods with per-role caps, AIM pool, maturity holdbacks, and BenchKit merit blending -- the 'econ splits/gauge' direction (KNOWN TENSION #4). It diverges from the current single-beneficiary MintCoordinated receipt reality. Not a defect of THIS repo (it is explicitly research and out of the production path), but it is the concrete locus where the splits-vs-single-beneficiary tension is being explored; nothing here is yet reflected in normative receipt/contract specs.

contradictions.title: Reputation/merit (BenchKit) blended directly into allocation weights
contradictions.detail: allocation_gauge applies BenchKit merit multipliers (reputation/poi/poinsight) to per-role allocation. North Star permits reputation to influence SELECTION & ALLOCATION (allowed here) but it MUST NOT override deterministic scoring/finality. This repo only affects allocation of an emissions pool and does not touch scoring -- consistent with the rule, but worth flagging as the place to keep that boundary explicit if formulas become canonical.

records.classification_rationale: Phase-1 scaffolding repo for AFI agent templates/manifests/spawning, not a protocol-defining or replay-critical component. records.classification_rationale: README.md:3-5 and package.json:3 describe this as "AFI's universal gateway framework for custom character development" / "universal gateway for multiple interfaces and integrations" — an ElizaOS-based external client + character framework. It is the ingest-boundary plane in the five-plane model. It is REFERENCE_IMPL (not pure SUPPORTING/DOCS) because it contains a working, deployable signal-ingest API (src/server.ts, src/http/app.ts) that exercises the protocol surface: it normalizes a signal payload into the canonical VaultedSignalRecord type (imported from afi-infra) and persists it. It does not define normative schemas/specs itself, but it consumes them, so it is one concrete implementation of the ingest spine rather than the canon.
records.primary_purpose: An ElizaOS-based universal gateway / framework: it lets community developers build custom characters with skills, provides AFI-specific Eliza plugins and HTTP/WS clients that call AFI services (afi-reactor Froggy pipeline), and exposes a tenant-scoped HTTP API (api-keys, skills discovery, and a /api/v1/signals ingest endpoint that writes into the TSSD vault).

contradictions.title: Stated client-only / no-direct-DB principle contradicted by direct Mongo TSSD vault writes on a live route
contradictions.detail: README and AGENTS assert this repo is a pure HTTP/WS client with 'No direct database access' and 'No AFI business logic here'. But src/http/app.ts POST /api/v1/signals calls vault.upsert(record) where vault = TenantScopedTSSDVaultClient -> MongoTSSDVaultClient (afi-infra), writing the canonical VaultedSignalRecord straight into Mongo (afi_tssd.tssd_signals). server.ts mounts buildApp() with the real Mongo-backed factory, so this is a deployable path (Railway-targeted, binds 0.0.0.0), not test-only.
contradictions.evidence: src/http/app.ts:133-136 'const vault = vaultFactory(tenantId); await vault.upsert(parsed.record);' + README.md:69 '4. No direct DB access — All AFI data access happens via AFI HTTP/WS APIs' + src/server.ts:56 'const { app, apiKeyStore } = buildApp();'

records.classification_rationale: This is the AFI governance/DAO plane, NOT one of the five canonical signal-data planes. README.md:13 "AFI Governance is the agentic decision-making layer of the AFI Protocol." It defines a governance "Universal Proposal Signal" schema plus Snapshot/Safe/Zodiac-Reality execution. Under the North Star, registries/governance "influence SELECTION & ALLOCATION only -- MUST NOT override deterministic scoring or protocol finality," so this repo is outside the normative scoring/commitment/evidence surface. It is classified REFERENCE_IMPL rather than NORMATIVE because (a) it picks a SPECIFIC governance stack (Snapshot off-chain + Safe multisig + Zodiac Reality module) as the execution path, and (b) nearly all logic is stubs/placeholders (validator/proposal_scorer.ts returns Math.random(); cli/submit-proposal.ts is a console.log; agents/proposal_executor_agent.ts and SAFE REALITY/safe.ts are empty bodies). It is not DOCS because it carries a (partially) machine-readable proposal schema + a working zod validation harness. It is not STALE overall (HEAD commit 2026-01-24), though it contains stale sub-artifacts (docs/governance_links.md, sprint_archive).
records.primary_purpose: Defines the AFI governance/DAO layer: a standardized "Universal Proposal Signal" schema for human/agent governance proposals, agent-validator scoring stubs, a deferred-staking activation threshold, and a hybrid off-chain (Snapshot) -> on-chain (Safe + Zodiac Reality) proposal execution model.

contradictions.title: 'Universal Proposal Signal' reuses canonical 'signal' vocabulary for a non-canonical concept
contradictions.detail: The repo treats governance proposals as 'signals' (signal_type: governance_proposal) and calls its schema the canonical 'Universal Proposal Signal,' colliding with the protocol-canonical USS v1.1 micro-signal (afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1/). These are different objects; conflating them risks an external integrator treating governance proposals as scoreable evidence signals or vice versa. SignalChallengeProposal further references real UWR fields (baseScore/decayScore/analystId/market) bridging governance into the scoring plane.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/UniversalProposalSignal.schema.json:5 '"signal_type": "governance_proposal"'; README.md:38-40 'Universal Proposal Signal ... follow a standardized schema'; SignalChallengeProposal.schema.json:62-91 baseScore/decayScore/analystId 'Original UWR score'

contradictions.title: Canonical schema file is not valid JSON (un-parseable as a schema)
contradictions.detail: schemas/UniversalProposalSignal.schema.json is declared the 'canonical schema' but is not machine-readable JSON: it begins with // comments, embeds prose paragraphs, numbered scoring-flow steps, and multiple unrelated JSON blocks (agent registry, FastAPI endpoints). An external validator cannot load it. The actual enforced schema is a hand-written zod object in the test harness, which can silently drift from the doc.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/UniversalProposalSignal.schema.json:1-2 '// Mirrors specs/... // Used internally'; specs/universal_proposal_schema.json:1-3 same; vs afi_proposal_schema_test/proposal_schema.test.ts:5-26 zod schema is the real validator

contradictions.title: Governance/staking gated on mint supply implies tight coupling to commitment plane that is not wired

records.classification_rationale: The brief explicitly names "afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts" and "TSSD spec" as NORMATIVE artifacts. This repo hosts the canonical VaultedSignalRecord type (src/tssd/types.ts:331), the TSSD_VAULT_SPEC (docs/TSSD_VAULT_SPEC.md) and TSSD_REPLAY_CLI_SPEC.v0.1 (docs/TSSD_REPLAY_CLI_SPEC.v0.1.md) which define the Evidence-plane vault schema, lifecycle semantics (RAW->ENRICHED->ANALYZED->SCORED->MINTED->REPLAYED, types.ts:10-16), public-vs-proprietary surface separation (types.ts:206-230), and replay determinism invariants. These are interoperability rules, not just one stack. However the repo MIXES normative type/spec definitions with a REFERENCE_IMPL (Mongo-only client) and STALE docs (AGENTS.md, .afi-codex.json), so it is normative-primary but impure.
records.primary_purpose: afi-infra defines AFI's canonical T.S.S.D. Vault types and lifecycle spec (the off-chain per-signal Evidence-plane record) plus signal-stage Zod schemas, agent-role definitions, and a read-only deterministic replay runner; package.json:3 "Infrastructure services, TSSD vault clients, and shared schemas for AFI Protocol".

The Evidence-plane reference impl collapses to Mongo-only, the exact 'Mongo tunnel vision' tension.

contradictions.title: Canonical vault type omits pinned-version / determinism replay fields required by north-star rule (2)

VaultedSignalRecord stores only optional analystScore.strategyVersion; there is no pinned DAG topology id, plugin-manifest version, scorer/codex version, or content-hash linkage field at the record level. The replay spec records scorer version at RUN time, not in the canonical document, weakening challenge-ready reproducibility.

records.classification_rationale: Pure TypeScript math primitives library with zero deps and no I/O: README.md:5 "a pure TypeScript library providing deterministic mathematical functions for time value of money, curve primitives, valuation models, and signal decay"; AGENTS.md:74 "Depends on: NONE (pure math library...)". It is consumed by reactor/core/mint/token (AGENTS.md:73) but defines no protocol schemas, dialects, or canonical type contracts. It is a shared utility (SUPPORTING) underpinning deterministic transforms rather than a normative spec or the reference spine itself. The one borderline element is src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts which calls itself "Canonical implementation" of the emissions model, but it is an off-chain numeric helper, not the on-chain authority, so it does not raise the whole repo to NORMATIVE.
records.primary_purpose: Pure, deterministic, dependency-free TypeScript library of financial/scoring math primitives (time value of money, curve shaping, reverse DCF/implied-rate, signal decay/half-life, and a three-phase emissions schedule) shared across AFI Protocol repos for scoring, tokenomics, and pipeline computation.
protocol_touchpoints: replay/determinism (pure deterministic transforms underpinning reproducible scoring)
DAG/pipeline (curve/decay transforms consumed by reactor scoring)
USS ingest (decay/greeks field mapping, docs-only reference)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: This library is Phase 1 ready and serves as the canonical math reference for the AFI ecosystem.
Canonical implementation of the three-phase front-loaded emissions model. Derived from AFI_Emissions_Final.wl (Wolfram Mathematica)
5. afi-token: Smart contracts and on-chain tokenomics ... This separation ensures that: Math functions can be audited independently

contradictions.title: Emissions cap/curve hardcoded despite 'no policy constants' rule
contradictions.detail: AGENTS.md forbids embedding AFI-specific policy constants and instructs passing them as arguments, but emissionsSchedule.ts ships DEFAULT_EMISSIONS_PARAMS with a hardcoded 86B cap, phase years, milestone fractions (1/3, 0.8, 1.0) and shape factors. These ARE policy/tokenomics constants. Mitigant: params are overridable via Partial input, so the constants are defaults not enforced values. Still a code/doc contradiction and a tokenomics-source ambiguity vs the on-chain emissions authority.
contradictions.evidence: AGENTS.md:92 'No hardcoded policy constants: AFI-specific parameters (e.g., decay rates, discount rates) should be passed as arguments, not embedded in functions.' vs src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts:59-73 'export const DEFAULT_EMISSIONS_PARAMS ... cap: 86_000_000_000n ... shapeEarly: 2.0'

contradictions.title: Off-chain emissions schedule presented as 'canonical' vs on-chain emissions caps being the commitment-plane authority
contradictions.detail: Per the portable-protocol north star, emissions caps / epoch linkage belong to the on-chain commitment plane (afi-token/afi-mint). This repo labels its JS schedule 'Canonical implementation' and exposes getRemainingSupply/getCumulativeEmissions which read like supply-accounting logic. If consumers treat this off-chain TS as the source of truth for caps rather than the contracts, it collapses commitment-plane authority into a utility lib. Should be clarified as off-chain simulation/derivation of the on-chain schedule.
contradictions.evidence: src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts:215-217 'export function getRemainingSupply(schedule, alreadyMinted){ return Math.max(0, Number(schedule.params.cap) - alreadyMinted); }'; AGENTS.md:157 'afi-token may reference emissions curves from afi-math (for off-chain simulation)'

contradictions.title: Stale / inconsistent consumer repo names
contradictions.detail: .afi-codex.json lists consumers using older names (afi-engine, afi-token-finalized, afi-plugins) while AGENTS.md uses current names (afi-core, afi-mint, afi-token). README likewise references afi-token-finalized and afi-engine. Mixed naming across the same repo's own metadata can drive wrong dependency/architecture decisions.
contradictions.evidence: .afi-codex.json:21-28 'consumers: [afi-engine, afi-token-finalized, afi-config, afi-plugins, afi-reactor, afi-core]'; README.md:182-185 'afi-engine: Signal processing... afi-token-finalized: Smart contracts'; AGENTS.md:6 'used across afi-reactor, afi-core, afi-mint, and afi-token'

notable_findings.title: Library is genuinely pure/deterministic — strong replay-supporting property
notable_findings.evidence: AGENTS.md:81 'All functions are referentially transparent (same inputs -> same outputs)'; AGENTS.md:133 forbids 'non-deterministic functions (e.g., random number generation, Date.now())'; src/index.ts:12 'All functions are deterministic and side-effect free.'
notable_findings.recommendation: Keep this guarantee; these decay/curve/emissions transforms are exactly the kind of pinned, version-recorded transforms the VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC depends on. If reactor/scoring pins plugin/analyst versions, the afi-math version (currently 0.1.0) should be among the pinned, replay-recorded versions so a different stack can reproduce scored outputs.

notable_findings.title: Emissions schedule module is the only protocol-policy-bearing code; floats used for token accounting
notable_findings.evidence: src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts:41 'Per-epoch emission amounts (in base units, not wei)' and emissions computed as JS number floats (line 144 'emissions = baseEmissions.map(e => e * scale)') while cap is bigint (line 60 'cap: 86_000_000_000n').
notable_findings.recommendation: Float emission amounts cannot be authoritative for on-chain token accounting (precision loss vs wei/bigint). Clarify in docs that this module is off-chain simulation/derivation only and that the on-chain afi-token contract is the canonical emissions cap/epoch authority. If outputs are ever anchored on-chain, define a deterministic float->integer (wei) quantization rule to preserve replay determinism.

records.classification_rationale: afi-mint is the off-chain minting *coordination* spine of the reference stack, not a protocol-normative source. package.json:3 "AFI signal validation and token minting coordination"; AGENTS.md:3 "coordinates signal-driven token minting... but does NOT contain token contracts or on-chain economics logic"; AGENTS.md:79 "afi-mint = minting coordination (off-chain logic...)". It implements one concrete spine: a Snapshot-governance appeal pipeline (ValidatorDaemon, SignalStateManager) and an emissions/goldpaper data provider, calling an interface IMintCoordinatorContract.mintForSignal() (MintExecutor.ts:32-37). The on-chain contracts here are empty stubs (contracts/MintManager.sol:4-5 "// Stub for minting logic"), and the canonical receipt/emissions definitions live elsewhere (afi-token contracts, afi-math). It is a working reference implementation, but a few artifacts (mint_receipt_schema.json, the signalId/epoch/beneficiary/receiptId/extraData breadcrumb shape) carry near-normative weight for on-chain<->off-chain linkage.
records.primary_purpose: Off-chain coordination of signal-driven AFI token minting: an appeal-based validator pipeline (auto qualify/reject by decay score, challenge window, Snapshot dispute voting) that finalizes signals and invokes an on-chain AFIMintCoordinator.mintForSignal() to emit a per-signal mint breadcrumb (signalId, epoch, beneficiary, receiptId).

contradictions.title: Two divergent SignalValidatorState state machines (Zod schema vs TS types)
contradictions.detail: The canonical TS state machine in src/orchestrator/types.ts uses kinds pending/qualified/rejected/challenge_window/contested/dispute_resolved/finalized/minted/rejected_final, while the Zod validation schema in schemas/SignalValidatorState.schema.ts uses a completely different, older set: pending/decay_pass/challenge_open/voting_complete/minted/rejected. An external validator using the published @afi-protocol/afi-mint/schemas Zod schema to validate state records would reject every record produced by the actual orchestrator. This breaks interoperability of the signal lifecycle dialect.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/SignalValidatorState.schema.ts:13-20 z.enum(['pending','decay_pass','challenge_open','voting_complete','minted','rejected']) vs src/orchestrator/types.ts:33-42 'pending'|'qualified'|'rejected'|'challenge_window'|'contested'|'dispute_resolved'|'finalized'|'minted'|'rejected_final'

contradictions.title: Reputation/governance can override deterministic mint outcome and amount
contradictions.detail: North-star rule 5 says reputation/registries/governance influence SELECTION & ALLOCATION only and MUST NOT override deterministic scoring or protocol finality. Here a Snapshot governance vote (DisputeResolver) can OVERTURN the validator's deterministic decay-score decision (qualified->reject / rejected->mint), directly flipping mint finality; and per-signal token amount is multiplied by reputationWeight and a governance-controlled epochPulseFactor. This conflates governance with deterministic scoring/finality.
contradictions.evidence: src/orchestrator/SignalStateManager.ts:284-286 'if (challengeSucceeded) { finalDecision = state.validatorDecision === \'qualified\' ? \'reject\' : \'mint\'; }'; src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:270-281 'R = metadata.reputationWeight ... adjustedAmount = baseAmount * this.config.epochPulseFactor'

contradictions.title: Non-deterministic constructs in a replay-critical mint path
contradictions.detail: Reference spine claims deterministic audit/replay (README.md:59 'deterministic audit trails'), but mint amount and state records embed wall-clock time (Date.now/new Date().toISOString throughout), and the emissions calc depends on mutable epoch-shared state (getEpochMintedAmount/getEpochTotalWeight) plus floating-point Math.exp/division before BigInt floor. Same inputs do not guarantee same scored/minted outputs across stacks unless time and epoch accumulation are snapshotted, which is not enforced here.
contradictions.evidence: src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:284 'BigInt(Math.floor(adjustedAmount * 10 ** this.config.decimals))'; :261-263 'getEpochTotalWeight(...) ... getEpochMintedAmount(...) ... remainingBudget'; src/orchestrator/SignalStateManager.ts:128 'new Date().toISOString()'

contradictions.title: On-chain commitment presented as full mint engine but contracts are empty stubs
contradictions.detail: README.md:44 advertises 'Solidity Contracts for On-Chain Mint Management' and contracts/ lists ChallengeRegistry/MintManager/ThresholdRules, yet all three are empty stub contracts. The actual on-chain commitment plane (mint events, supply caps, receipts) lives in afi-token. This matches the 'BASE = full ledger (vision) vs receipt-only (reality)' tension and risks readers treating these stubs as the commitment layer.
contradictions.evidence: contracts/MintManager.sol:4-5 'contract MintManager { // Stub for minting logic }'; contracts/ThresholdRules.sol:4 '// Stub for DAO-approved minting thresholds'; contracts/ChallengeRegistry.sol:4 '// Stub for handling signal mint challenges'

contradictions.title: Single-beneficiary mint model, no econ splits / gauge
contradictions.detail: MintRequest carries exactly one beneficiary and one tokenAmount per signal; there is no split/gauge/multi-beneficiary distribution anywhere in the repo. This is consistent with the known 'single beneficiary mint vs econ splits/gauge' tension and means the reference coordinator cannot express revenue/emission splits described in broader econ vision.
contradictions.evidence: src/orchestrator/MintExecutor.ts:19-27 'interface MintRequest { beneficiary: string; tokenAmount: bigint; ... }'; grep for split|gauge|beneficiaries returned no logic hits

records.classification_rationale: Repo is a Phase-1 scaffolding-only operations/devops toolkit: local deploy stubs, health-check shell scripts, runbooks, SLO docs, and AgentOps helpers (config hashing, agent versioning, Sentry). records.classification_rationale: A thin TypeScript scaffolding package (README.md:11 "intentionally thin and lightweight... NOT production logic"; package.json:3 "Plugin registry and template pack for AFI Protocol"). It defines local plugin types and a hardcoded in-memory registry of stub plugins (signal/analyzer/scorer/validator/executor/observer), all explicitly STUB-only returning empty/zero results (.afi-codex.json:73 "stubImplementationsOnly": true). It is the reference extension surface for the reference spine (afi-reactor DAG + afi-core ElizaOS runtime), not a normative protocol artifact: it carries no canonical schemas, no contracts, no determinism/replay rules, and its PluginManifest interface is a local TS type unconnected to the normative plugin-manifest.schema.json in afi-config. Classified REFERENCE_IMPL rather than SUPPORTING because it ships executable runtime extension code wired to reactor/core/eliza, and rather than NORMATIVE because nothing here is an interop rule other repos must validate against.
records.primary_purpose: Central plugin registry and template/stub pack that defines the extension surface (types, manifests, and placeholder implementations for signal generators, analyzers, scorers, validators, executors, observers) for the AFI reference stack (afi-reactor DAG orchestrator and afi-core ElizaOS runtime), with all real logic deferred to runtime/infra repos.

records.classification_rationale: afi-reactor is a concrete TypeScript implementation of ONE scoring/orchestration spine (the "reference spine" gateway->reactor DAG->vault per North Star). package.json declares `"name": "afi-reactor"` with mongodb, express, zod and `file:../afi-core`/`file:../afi-factory` deps (package.json:1-46). It ships a working flexible plugin DAG (src/dag/DAGBuilder.ts, DAGExecutor.ts, plugins/nodes), enrichment, USS/CPJ ingest mappers, and a Mongo persistence service. It is NOT itself a normative spec: its DAG/replay docs explicitly defer schema ownership to other repos (config/schema.codex.json `ownerRepo` fields point to afi-core/afi-infra; VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:21 "canonical vaulted-signal schema is owned by afi-infra"). The one genuinely normative artifact it carries, VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md, is a spec doc bundled inside a reference impl. The repo's own framing ("ONLY orchestrator", "canonical orchestrator", "The DAG is law") presents the reference impl as protocol law, which is the P1 tension rather than a reason to reclassify.
records.primary_purpose: afi-reactor is the reference TypeScript orchestrator that runs a flexible, plugin-based DAG pipeline to ingest (USS v1.1 / CPJ v0.1), enrich, deterministically score, and persist financial signals to a Mongo-backed scored-signal vault, exposing replay/simulation entrypoints.

reference_only_assumptions.quote: **afi-reactor is the ONLY orchestrator in AFI Protocol.** All canonical pipelines, DAGs, and routing logic live here.

### 3. The DAG is law
Every signal path (ingest → enrich → score → mint/review) must be expressible as a Reactor DAG; ad-hoc flows are anti-patterns.
**afi-reactor** is the canonical DAG orchestrator for AFI Protocol... This is the **ONLY orchestrator** in the AFI ecosystem—agents are nodes, not orchestrators.
Provides MongoDB persistence for scored signals from AFI Reactor. ... AFI_MONGO_URI: MongoDB connection string (required)
"description": "Stores approved signals in the MongoDB T.S.S.D. Vault."
"meta": { "bsonType": "object", "required": ["signalId", "timestamp", "score"] ...} (vaulted-signal schema expressed only as MongoDB BSON)

contradictions.title: DAG executor uses non-deterministic ordering/IDs, risking replay determinism the spec mandates
contradictions.detail: VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md makes determinism normative (same codex+config+data => same outputs). But DAGExecutor relies on Set iteration order, Math.random execution IDs, and Promise.all parallel races for same-level nodes; with non-commutative node side effects on shared PipelineState this could yield order-dependent outputs. Node-level transforms (TA, CPJ hashing, novelty) are individually deterministic, but the orchestration layer offers no pinned topology hash or ordered merge guarantee.
contradictions.evidence: src/dag/DAGExecutor.ts:1002-1004 generateExecutionId uses Date.now()+Math.random; DAGExecutor.ts:716-743 executeLevelParallel runs Promise.all over a level; vs docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:37 "Same codex version + same config + same data snapshot → same validator outputs."

contradictions.title: DAG spec/codex describe an aspirational 15-node design not matched by implemented nodes; placeholder schema entry present
contradictions.detail: AFI_REACTOR_DAG_SPEC.md documents a 15-node 'target design' (market-data-streamer, onchain-feed-ingestor, exchange-execution-node, etc.) while src/dag implements a different smaller node set (Analyst/Execution/Observer + Scout/News/Sentiment/Pattern/SignalIngress/TechnicalIndicators/AiMl). schema.codex.json contains a filler entry to pad the count. This makes the canonical config a poor source of truth for an external implementer.
contradictions.evidence: config/schema.codex.json:289-291 "additional-schema.schema.json" ... "Additional schema to reach count of 15."; docs/AFI_REACTOR_DAG_SPEC.md:28 "5 primary categories with 15 total nodes in the current target design"

notable_findings.title: Vault persistence is Mongo-only and canonical contract is BSON-typed; violates multi-engine evidence plane
notable_findings.evidence: src/services/tssdVaultService.ts:22 "import { MongoClient, Db, Collection } from \"mongodb\";"; config/schema.codex.json:49-53 vaulted-signal meta uses bsonType; config/dag.codex.json:196 "persists execution result to MongoDB vault."
notable_findings.recommendation: Define vaulted-signal as an engine-neutral JSON Schema (current vault.schema.json North Star), with Mongo BSON as one binding. Make the reactor write through a storage-agnostic interface so PostgreSQL/Timescale/Influx backends can satisfy the same contract.

notable_findings.title: Strong per-node determinism + canonical hashing/ID generation for replay (positive)
notable_findings.evidence: src/uss/cpjMapper.ts:198-226 deterministic SHA256 ingest hash with key sorting + array canonicalization; src/novelty/canonicalNovelty.ts:9-12 "CanonicalNovelty contains ONLY replay-stable fields (no timestamps)"; enrichment/technicalIndicators.ts:2-5 deterministic
notable_findings.recommendation: Preserve and promote: these canonicalization rules (CPJ ingest hash, replay-stable novelty, deterministic signalIds) are exactly the determinism artifacts a portable protocol needs. Consider extracting the canonicalization algorithm to a shared normative spec so other stacks reproduce identical hashes.

notable_findings.title: Orchestration layer lacks pinned topology + parallel-merge determinism guarantee
notable_findings.evidence: src/dag/DAGExecutor.ts:1002-1004 generateExecutionId via Date.now()+Math.random(); DAGExecutor.ts:734-735 "const promises = chunk.map... await Promise.all(promises)"; spec requires determinism docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:37
notable_findings.recommendation: Add a pinned topology hash (codexVersion + node/plugin/analyst/validator versions) and an order-independent state merge (or canonical post-merge sort) so same-level parallel nodes cannot perturb scored outputs. Record the pinned version set in each replay session.

records.classification_rationale: This is a Next.js marketing/brochure website built on the purchased "Axleo" ThemeForest template (package.json name "axleo-next", README:3 "AFI Research Institute website (Next.js), adapted from the Axleo template"). It is the public portal for research programs, services, team, blog, and a fiat-only Stripe "Adopt-an-Agent" donation flow (README:26-33). It contains zero protocol code: no schemas, no contracts, no TSSD/vault/mint/DAG logic, no API routes (find for api dir returned 0), and the README explicitly states it is separate from the AFI Network ("Keep a clear separation from the AFI Network (registry/explorer live elsewhere; we link out when ready)" README:33). The repo carries no normative, reference-implementation, or replay-relevant artifacts and therefore sits outside the portable-protocol surface.

records.classification_rationale: This is a versioned library of agent "skills" (markdown docs with YAML front-matter describing typed I/O, risk levels, determinism flags, and golden eval cases), plus TypeScript tooling (Zod schema, linter, manifest builder) and agent skillsets. README.md:28 calls it "the canonical source of truth for AFI agent capabilities" and .afi-codex.json:3 role is "Canonical, versioned library of AFI Agent Skills". It is consumed by afi-core/afi-reactor/afi-factory (AGENTS.md:55) and depends on afi-config (.afi-codex.json:15). It defines an agent-capability registry, not protocol interoperability rules. It is "canonical" only for the skill front-matter contract (docs/skill_contract_v1.md), not for USS/CPJ/vault/pipeline/contract schemas. It carries no replay-critical artifacts itself: skills like vault-replay-determinism and receipt-verification only describe expected behavior/I-O for agents; the normative determinism rules live elsewhere (VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC, afi-config schemas). Hence SUPPORTING (agent-capability descriptions feeding the reference spine) rather than NORMATIVE, REFERENCE_IMPL, or DOCS.
records.primary_purpose: A canonical, versioned library of AFI agent "skills" -- discrete reusable capabilities defined as markdown + YAML front-matter (typed I/O, risk level, determinism flag, golden evals) with TS tooling (Zod schema, linter, manifest builder) and agent skillsets, consumed by afi-core/afi-reactor/afi-factory.

reference_only_assumptions.quote: `tssd:read`: Read-only TSSD access; `tssd:write`: Write access to TSSD (requires justification); `codex:replay`: Historical event replay

contradictions.title: TSSD bound to a single named engine ('Time-Series Signal Database')
contradictions.detail: skills/README.md expands the tssd:read tool token as 'TSSD (Time-Series Signal Database)'. The portable protocol treats the evidence/vault plane as multi-engine (Mongo/PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB/InfluxDB per vault.schema.json). Here TSSD appears only as an opaque tool-permission token (good), but the parenthetical gloss implies one fixed time-series store. Minor/documentation-level: no code mandates an engine.
contradictions.evidence: skills/README.md:86 '`["tssd:read"]`: Read-only access to TSSD (Time-Series Signal Database)'

contradictions.title: Determinism declared as protocol-critical but not enforced by tooling (TODO stub)
contradictions.detail: Skills set determinism_required:true and the docs claim golden cases enable 'consensus verification' and 'bit-for-bit' replay, yet the linter explicitly leaves determinism checking as a TODO and only prints an informational note; it never validates that golden cases pass or that no Date.now()/Math.random() is used. A replay-relevant guarantee is asserted in docs but unimplemented in the gate.
contradictions.evidence: scripts/lint-skills.ts:82-84 'if (fm.determinism_required) { // TODO: Add more determinism checks (e.g., no Date.now(), Math.random()) console.log("  ℹ️  Determinism required: ...") }'
