records.classification_rationale: A self-contained Python/FastAPI AI/ML microservice ("Tiny Brains") that provides ML enrichment predictions for the Froggy lane and is explicitly invoked by the reference spine (afi-reactor) over HTTP. README.md:3 "AFI's AI/ML microservice for Froggy enrichment pipeline"; README.md:11 "This service is called by `afi-reactor` via the Tiny Brains client". It implements ONE pluggable scoring/enrichment helper inside the Scoring-DAG/Evidence plane: no schemas, no contracts, no vault/TSSD store, no mint/receipt logic, no replay/canon artifacts. It is a candidate transform within the DAG, not a protocol rule, so REFERENCE_IMPL (a concrete pluggable component of the reference spine) rather than NORMATIVE.
records.primary_purpose: A FastAPI microservice exposing POST /predict/froggy that runs a three-brain ensemble (Chronos time-series, HMM regime, LightGBM meta-learner) over Froggy enrichment context and returns a FroggyAiMlV1 prediction (convictionScore/direction/regime/riskFlag/notes), with heuristic fallbacks so it never errors.
protocol_touchpoints: replay/determinism (model versions/seeds at inference not pinned in outputs)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: This service is called by `afi-reactor` via the Tiny Brains client (`src/aiMl/tinyBrainsClient.ts`) when Froggy enrichment has `aiMl.enabled = true`.
Mirrors TinyBrainsFroggyInput from src/aiMl/tinyBrainsClient.ts
Mirrors FroggyAiMlV1 from afi-core/analysts/froggy.enrichment_adapter.ts
X-AFI-Client: afi-reactor-froggy-v1

contradictions.title: I/O contract hand-mirrored from afi-reactor TS types, not validated against afi-config canonical schemas
contradictions.detail: Input/output Pydantic models are documented as manual mirrors of TypeScript types in afi-reactor/afi-core rather than generated from or validated against the canonical USS/CPJ/afi-config schemas. This duplicates the ingest/scoring dialect by hand and risks silent drift from the canonical schema surface, weakening the rule that all data on the protocol surface should validate against afi-config schemas. It is reference-impl scope (not a production normative path), hence P2.
contradictions.evidence: tiny_brains_service/models.py:12 "# Input Models (mirror TinyBrainsFroggyInput from src/aiMl/tinyBrainsClient.ts)"; models.py:90 "Mirrors FroggyAiMlV1 from afi-core/analysts/froggy.enrichment_adapter.ts"

contradictions.title: Inference is non-deterministic / model versions not pinned in output (replay rule)
contradictions.detail: Training scripts pin seeds (random_state=42, seed=42) but the serving path does not record or pin model id/version/hash in its output, and the Chronos path uses stochastic sampling (num_samples=10) on a synthetically-constructed price series with no fixed inference RNG. Per determinism + pinned-transforms replay rules, a scored output produced through this transform would not be reproducible from inputs alone, nor self-describing about which model version produced it (only a free-text 'notes' string distinguishes model vs heuristic). The reactor/vault layer must capture this; the service does not surface it. Scoped P2 since this component is pluggable and the canon lives upstream.
contradictions.evidence: tiny_brains_service/brains/chronos_brain.py:146 "num_samples=10,  # Generate multiple samples for uncertainty"; service.py:131 notes only "final_notes = \" | \".join(notes_parts)"

upstream: afi-reactor (HTTP caller via src/aiMl/tinyBrainsClient.ts; provides run scripts/.env config)

notable_findings.title: No commitment/evidence/analytics persistence: pure stateless transform
notable_findings.evidence: Pattern searches for vault|TSSD|VaultedSignalRecord|mongodb|postgresql|timescaledb|influxdb|kafka|warehouse|snowflake returned ZERO hits across .py/.md/.toml. Only persistence is local model files: .gitignore "models/*.joblib ... models/*.txt".
notable_findings.recommendation: Confirm this stays a stateless scoring helper; it does not and should not collapse commitment/evidence/analytics planes. No persistence-store coupling exists, so no multi-engine tension here.

notable_findings.title: No on-chain / mint / receipt / emissions surface
notable_findings.evidence: Pattern searches for mintForSignal|coordinateMint|beneficiary|epoch|AFIMintCoordinator|AFISignalReceipt|EmissionsMinted|MintCoordinated returned ZERO hits.
notable_findings.recommendation: None. This repo is entirely off-chain enrichment; it is correctly outside the Commitment plane.

notable_findings.title: No USS/CPJ ingest dialect usage; signalId carried only as opaque string
notable_findings.evidence: Searches for usignal|USS|cpj|CPJ returned ZERO hits. models.py:72 only "signalId: str" and traceId, with no reference to USS v1.1 / CPJ v0.1 envelopes.
notable_findings.recommendation: Document that the /predict/froggy payload is a reactor-internal enrichment sub-contract, NOT a canonical ingest dialect, to avoid future readers treating TinyBrainsFroggyInput as a protocol entry dialect.

notable_findings.title: No replay/determinism/codex machinery present
notable_findings.evidence: Searches for replay|ReplaySession|determinism|deterministic|codex returned ZERO hits. Service is fail-soft and stateless (service.py:144 except -> neutral fallback).
notable_findings.recommendation: Replay canon and pinned-topology recording must live in the reactor/vault layer; this service should be made to echo model id/version/hash so the upstream replay record can pin its transform version.

notable_findings.title: Output regime/direction vocabulary is internal, not schema-anchored
notable_findings.evidence: hmm_brain.py:118 state_map produces "calm_bull"/"panic_bear"/"choppy_neutral"/"unknown" while models.py:102 regime is a free-form Optional[str]; README examples also use "calm_bull". No canonical enum is referenced.
notable_findings.recommendation: If regime labels flow into scored/vaulted records, define a canonical enum in afi-config and validate against it; otherwise tag as reference-impl-internal vocabulary.

notable_findings.title: Fail-soft neutral fallback can mask scoring failures in a deterministic pipeline
notable_findings.evidence: service.py:148-153 returns convictionScore=0.5/direction=neutral on ANY exception; README.md:21-22 "Service always returns a valid response (never errors)".
notable_findings.recommendation: For challenge-ready/reproducible evidence, the upstream reactor should distinguish a genuine neutral prediction from an error-induced neutral (the 'notes' field carries this, but the structured fields do not), so a replay cannot silently diverge on transient failures.

records.primary_purpose: afi-token implements the AFI Protocol's on-chain Commitment plane on BASE: an xERC20-based ERC-20 emissions token (AFIToken, 86B hard cap), an ERC-1155 provenance receipt (AFISignalReceipt), and an AFIMintCoordinator orchestrator that links token+receipt mints to off-chain signal/epoch metadata via the MintCoordinated event.
records.classification_rationale: The repo's Solidity contracts (src/*.sol) are listed in the North Star as NORMATIVE commitment-plane artifacts, yet the repo as a whole is a concrete BASE/xERC20/Foundry reference deployment with a SPECIFIC stack (defi-wonderland xERC20, OpenZeppelin AccessControl, single Treasury Safe role pattern). README.md:3 'AFI Protocol token implementation with xERC20 cross-chain compatibility ... on Base.' The on-chain shape (breadcrumb events, off-chain DAG/TSSD policy) is the reference spine, not a portable rule set. The .sol sources themselves are normative anchors; surrounding TS/docs are reference-impl scaffolding. Net classification: REFERENCE_IMPL containing embedded normative contract artifacts.

reference_only_assumptions.quote: mintEmissions is the ONLY emissions entrypoint, gated by EMISSIONS_ROLE. This role is granted to the MintCoordinator contract, which enforces off-chain policy (DAG/TSSD, Epoch Pulse, signal provenance)
Built on defi-wonderland's xERC20 standard ... Initially deployed on Base as the canonical chain
Pattern A: Both admin and emissions controller are the same Treasury Safe ... address admin_ = treasurySafe; address emissionsController_ = treasurySafe;
Every token minted via emissions is logged in the `.afi-codex.json` stream
All agents operating in AFI Protocol repos must follow afi-config/codex/governance/droids/AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md ... the Charter wins

contradictions.title: On-chain mint is single-beneficiary; no econ split / gauge despite agentic-reward vision
contradictions.detail: MintCoordinator.mintForSignal mints the full tokenAmount to a single req.beneficiary with no split/gauge/multi-payee logic. The portable-protocol KNOWN TENSION (econ splits/gauge vs single beneficiary mint) is realized here as single-beneficiary. This is acceptable as a reference impl but should be marked reference-vs-normative since docs imply richer reward distribution.
contradictions.evidence: src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:76 'token.mintEmissions(req.beneficiary, req.tokenAmount);' (single beneficiary, no split)

contradictions.title: Decentralization claims contradict single-Safe admin+emissions role concentration
contradictions.detail: README markets 'No centralized control'. The mainnet deploy (Pattern A) assigns BOTH DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE and EMISSIONS_ROLE to one Treasury Safe, which can mint up to the 86B cap and grant/revoke all roles. This contradicts the decentralization framing and the North Star rule that reputation/registries influence selection/allocation only.
contradictions.evidence: README.md:225 '✅ No centralized control' vs script/DeployAFITokenMainnet.s.sol:62-63 'address admin_ = treasurySafe; address emissionsController_ = treasurySafe;'

contradictions.title: Local SignalSchema.zod.ts is not the canonical USS v1.1 dialect
contradictions.detail: Decentralization rule (1) requires the canonical dialect validate vs afi-config schemas. This repo defines its own bespoke Zod SignalSchema (id, provider_id, content, score, novelty_score) with no reference to USS v1.1 / afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1. USS|CPJ strings are entirely absent from the repo. Reward math (emissions_with_novelty.ts) parses against this local schema, risking dialect drift from the normative ingest boundary.
contradictions.evidence: models/SignalSchema.zod.ts:3-15 'export const SignalSchema = z.object({ id, provider_id, content, timestamp, score, novelty_score ... })' (grep for usignal|USS|cpj returns zero hits)

contradictions.title: Replay/'Codex replayable' claim unbacked on-chain; receipt is breadcrumb only
contradictions.detail: README:227 asserts 'Codex replayable & auditable' and docs claim every mint is logged to .afi-codex.json with novelty/challenge history, but on-chain only signalId/epoch/amounts/beneficiary are anchored (no payload hash, no UWR axes, no pinned-version anchor). Replay determinism depends entirely on off-chain afi-mint/afi-reactor. Matches CURRENT ON-CHAIN REALITY (breadcrumb, not dense brain); aspirational richer anchors not present.
contradictions.evidence: src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:84-85 'emit MintCoordinated(req.signalId, req.epoch, req.beneficiary, req.tokenAmount, req.receiptAmount);' vs README.md:227 '✅ Codex replayable & auditable'

contradictions.title: Stale toolchain and entrypoint metadata
contradictions.detail: .droid.json declares toolchain 'hardhat' and preferred_chain 'sepolia' while the repo is Foundry-based on Base (foundry.toml, README). .afi-codex.json lists a non-existent entrypoint src/AFICoordinator.sol (actual file is src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol). agentops notes reference stale repo name afi-agents. .aider.conf.json repo_name is 'afi-core'.
contradictions.evidence: .droid.json:4 '"toolchain": "hardhat"'; .afi-codex.json:8 '"src/AFICoordinator.sol"' (file does not exist); .aider.conf.json:31 '"repo_name": "afi-core"'

downstream: afi-mint (consumes contracts; .droid.json: integrate with afi-mint mint_trigger.ts; calls EMISSIONS_ROLE on coordinator)

notable_findings.title: On-chain anchor matches North Star breadcrumb model (signalId/epoch/amounts only)
notable_findings.evidence: src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:38-44 'event MintCoordinated(bytes32 indexed signalId, uint256 indexed epochId, address indexed beneficiary, uint256 tokenAmount, uint256 receiptAmount);'
notable_findings.recommendation: Affirm in afi-docs that this minimal receipt is the canonical commitment surface; track aspirational payload-hash anchor as a separate normative extension.

notable_findings.title: 86B hard supply cap enforced on-chain as immutable constant
notable_findings.evidence: src/AFIToken.sol:35,97-99 'uint256 public constant TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP = 86_000_000_000 * 1e18; ... if (totalSupply() + amount > TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP) revert CapExceeded();'
notable_findings.recommendation: Record emissions cap as a normative commitment-plane invariant in the protocol surface spec.

notable_findings.title: DAG/TSSD/Epoch Pulse described as off-chain policy outside the token contract
notable_findings.evidence: src/AFIToken.sol:19-21 'Epoch-specific logic and DAG/TSSD integration are handled at the coordinator/off-chain policy layers, NOT in this token contract.'
notable_findings.recommendation: Good separation of commitment vs scoring DAG plane; keep DAG framework pluggable (reactor is reference, not mandatory).

notable_findings.title: EMISSIONS_ROLE concentration is the only gate on minting
notable_findings.evidence: src/AFIToken.sol:92 'function mintEmissions(address beneficiary, uint256 amount) external onlyRole(EMISSIONS_ROLE)' with mainnet Pattern A granting EMISSIONS_ROLE to the Treasury Safe (DeployAFITokenMainnet.s.sol:63)
notable_findings.recommendation: Document that protocol finality / determinism must be enforced off-chain by afi-mint before this role mints; an external validator cannot verify scoring legitimacy from on-chain data alone. Clarify trust assumption in VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.

notable_findings.title: No multi-engine vault / no analytics layer present (and none should be) but no USS/CPJ ingest validation either
notable_findings.evidence: grep usignal|USS|cpj => 0 hits; grep kafka|warehouse|postgresql|timescaledb|influxdb => 0 hits; models/SignalSchema.zod.ts:3 bespoke local schema
notable_findings.recommendation: Correctly scoped to commitment plane (no vault/analytics here). However the local Zod SignalSchema and reward math should validate against canonical USS v1.1 from afi-config rather than a private schema, or be explicitly marked illustrative/non-canonical.

notable_findings.title: ERC-1155 receipt metadata (URI) is a TODO placeholder for mainnet
notable_findings.evidence: script/DeployAFITokenMainnet.s.sol:103 '"https://afi.protocol/receipts/{id}.json", // TODO: Update to production URI'
notable_findings.recommendation: Resolve receipt URI before mainnet; if receipts are to carry provenance, define a canonical metadata schema (content hash linkage) in afi-config.

notable_findings.title: Stale/placeholder TS artifacts and inconsistent cross-repo references
notable_findings.evidence: config/emissions_config.ts:1 '// Pseudocode implementing AFII/AAG-based exponential decay with halving logic.'; proposals/era_transition_proposal.schema.json:1 '// Schema for submitting formal SES/AFII era change proposals.' (stub only)
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag emissions_config.ts / proposal schemas as pseudocode/stubs or remove; they imply functionality not present and could mislead replay/econ readers.

notable_findings.title: Codex/registry replay claims live in docs only, not enforced by code
notable_findings.evidence: docs/sections/agent_registry_&_codex_logging.md:3-7 'Every token minted via emissions is logged in the `.afi-codex.json` stream—capturing ... Novelty score ... Challenge history ... Final emission value'
notable_findings.recommendation: Either extract codex logging schema to a normative spec (afi-config) with reproducibility guarantees, or relabel as aspirational. Current .afi-codex.json is a repo manifest, not a per-mint provenance stream.

notable_findings.title: MintCoordinator does not grant itself receipt/token roles; relies on manual post-deploy wiring
notable_findings.evidence: script/DeployAFITokenMainnet.s.sol:140-143 'From Treasury Safe, grant roles: token.grantRole(EMISSIONS_ROLE, coordinator); receipts.grantRole(MINT_COORDINATOR_ROLE, coordinator); coordinator.grantRole(EMISSIONS_ROLE, <emissions_agent>)'
notable_findings.recommendation: Reference-impl operational detail; document the canonical role topology so external implementers can reproduce the commitment wiring on a different chain.

records.classification_rationale: This is a vendored fork of the third-party defi-wonderland/xERC20 (Connext xTokens) cross-chain bridged-token standard, not an AFI protocol artifact. package.json identifies it: name 'xerc20', homepage 'https://github.com/defi-wonderland/xERC20#readme', repository 'git+https://github.com/defi-wonderland/xERC20.git', author 'Wonderland' (package.json:2-12). The audit doc is 'Connext_xTokens_Audit_-_Creed_-_v0.3.pdf' (audits/). Grep for every AFI protocol term (afi, signalId, usignal/USS, cpj/CPJ, reactor, gateway, vault, TSSD, epoch, beneficiary, emissions, mintForSignal, coordinateMint, reputation, registry-as-AFI, replay, determinism, warehouse, kafka, mongo, postgres, doctrine) across all .sol/.md/.json/.ts returns ZERO hits in source. The single AFI-specific change is a one-line OZ v5 Ownable compatibility patch (commit 305922f) whose message states 'Enables AFI Protocol contracts to compile with OZ v5'. The contracts implement generic XERC20 bridge mint/burn rate-limits (XERC20.sol), unrelated to AFI signal->mint commitment semantics. It is infrastructure plumbing the AFI token layer may consume, but it contains no normative AFI surface.
records.primary_purpose: A vendored fork of the defi-wonderland/xERC20 (Connext xTokens) Solidity standard providing a cross-chain bridged-token implementation (XERC20, XERC20Lockbox, XERC20Factory) with per-bridge rate-limited mint/burn, patched only for OpenZeppelin v5 Ownable compatibility so it can compile alongside AFI Protocol contracts.
protocol_touchpoints: contracts (generic XERC20 bridge token, NOT AFI mint/receipt)
potentially upstream of mint/commitment plane only by reuse of OZ v5 toolchain (no direct AFI mint logic)

upstream: @openzeppelin/contracts (lib/openzeppelin-contracts, branch v4.9.3 per .gitmodules but patched for v5 per commit 305922f)
downstream: AFI commitment/mint layer (afi-token / afi-mint) MAY consume this xERC20 standard for a cross-chain bridgeable AFI token; relationship is implied only by the OZ-v5 patch commit message, no code import exists in this repo

notable_findings.title: Repo is an unmodified third-party Connext/defi-wonderland xERC20 standard, not an AFI artifact
notable_findings.evidence: package.json:6-12 "homepage": "https://github.com/defi-wonderland/xERC20#readme" ... "url": "git+https://github.com/defi-wonderland/xERC20.git" ... "author": "Wonderland"; audits/Connext_xTokens_Audit_-_Creed_-_v0.3.pdf
notable_findings.recommendation: Treat as OUT_OF_SCOPE for the portable-protocol surface; document in the repo map that afi-xerc20 is a vendored external standard (bridge token), not AFI normative/reference code, to prevent it being mistaken for the AFI mint/commitment plane.

notable_findings.title: Zero AFI protocol vocabulary present anywhere in source
notable_findings.evidence: grep -rni for afi|signalId|usignal|USS|cpj|reactor|gateway|vault|TSSD|epoch|beneficiary|emissions|mintForSignal|coordinateMint|MintCoordinated|AFIMintCoordinator|AFISignalReceipt|EmissionsMinted|replay|determinism|warehouse|kafka|mongo|postgres|doctrine across *.sol/*.md/*.json/*.ts returned no hits (only generic 'canonical token' for bridging in README.md:9 and XERC20Deploy.sol:25).
notable_findings.recommendation: Confirms classification: this holds no USS/CPJ ingest, TSSD/vault, AFI mint-receipt, DAG, replay, or registry surface. No normative AFI artifacts to extract.

notable_findings.title: Only AFI-specific change is a 1-line OZ v5 Ownable compatibility patch
notable_findings.evidence: git commit 305922f: 'chore: patch XERC20 for OpenZeppelin Ownable v5 compatibility ... Enables AFI Protocol contracts to compile with OZ v5'; XERC20.sol:37 constructor adds Ownable(msg.sender) then _transferOwnership(_factory).
notable_findings.recommendation: This is the sole AFI integration intent. If the AFI mint/commitment token is meant to be cross-chain via xERC20, document that linkage explicitly in afi-token/afi-mint; otherwise this fork may be a stray/exploratory dependency.

notable_findings.title: .gitmodules pins OZ v4.9.3 but contract was patched for OZ v5 -- toolchain inconsistency
notable_findings.evidence: .gitmodules: [submodule "lib/openzeppelin-contracts"] url ... branch = v4.9.3; contradicts commit 305922f which patches for 'OZ v5 which mandates initialOwner parameter' (XERC20.sol:37).
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag stale: reconcile the OZ submodule pin (v4.9.3) with the v5 compatibility patch, or the v5 patch will not match the vendored v4.9.3 lib at build time. Low impact (no AFI normative surface).

notable_findings.title: BASE (chain 8453) and 12 other chains contain broadcast artifacts for upstream XERC20Factory, not AFI mint contracts
notable_findings.evidence: broadcast/XERC20FactoryDeploy.sol/8453/run-latest.json: "contractName": "XERC20Factory", "contractAddress": "0xb913bE186110B1119d5B9582F316f142c908fc25"; same factory address across chains (XERC20Deploy.sol:41).
notable_findings.recommendation: Do not mistake these on-chain breadcrumbs for AFI commitment-plane (mint events/receipts/emissions). They are generic bridge-factory deployments inherited from the upstream fork; not part of the AFI signalId/epoch/content-hash anchoring model.
